step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the previous consensus. The middle constrictor muscle indeed originates from the body of the hyoid bone, which serves as an important anchor point during swallowing and other functions involving the pharyngeal region. This origin helps stabilize the position of the larynx and aids in creating negative pressure necessary for phonation and deglutition processes.
